title: My Cycling & Running Gear - For Big One Day Trips

transcript.text: May 1st, snow on the ground.
Hello, this is me showing my gear for the running and the biking, for the big biking
And the big running.
So for running, this is my running thing, this is called the Go Light because it's really
Light and it's rated to 12 power bars which means you can put 12 power bars in here, that's
How many power bars I have in here.
Each power bar is 240 calories, so 240 times 12, what is that, like 2,000 some odd calories?
That's enough for I guess a big run, I don't know how big because I haven't done it yet,
But I will soon.
And I can carry one water bottle on this sucker, on this side, and then on this side, here zoom
In on this, I have, this is my water purifier.
So all I have to do is when I come across a river, I just fill this sucker up and I drink
As much as I want and then I can fill the rest up in my power bar.
And then I put this sucker back on, and then I throw it back in here, and so I've got enough
Water to go as much as I want all day and I've got my 12 power bars, so I'm good to go.
And as far as warmth, I just tie everything else, I guess, I need to put it on my body
Around my waist.
And there you go, now, for the biking, this is my biking thing, I just attached this to
My seat post.
I'm hoping that my road bike won't break again when I put it on, I think my mountain
Bike will be alright.
It is kind of heavy, but it's not totally heavy.
I have a back, I have a light on here, I'm going to fix it with a cable, but anyways,
This is a bag that's like 20 years old, it was my dad's back in the day, and I just got
The zippers fixed, and I have it permanently attached to the rack, this aluminum rack with
A really strong like fishing line or whatever, just sewed all over so I can ride like mountain
Bike really hard and it bounced and not really getting loose or anything.
And so I have these two bags, and then this bag back here, I think in this bag back here
I'll put like my electrolytes, and then in the side here is where I put the power bars.
Now each one of these can hold 15 power bars.
So that's 30 power bars I can put on this, which is like 7,400 calories, so you're good
For all day, I don't have to stop at a store or anything, I can just go, and keep going.
And I can have two water bottles on that, and when I do go on rides, I just, I'll bring
This sucker along with me, and I'll just keep it stored in there so when I come across rivers
I can just fill up my water bottles with this really quickly, I don't have to go into a store
Or anything.
Now inside is where I'll store all of my clothing, I have this windbreaker, and I think
I'll get a more portable one when I get the money.
But I have this guy, which is a, which is something that'll, is good for keeping you warm.
It's like a bike jersey, it's got the pockets in the back and everything.
Yeah, you bring the leggings, you bring the arm warmers, which I don't have here, but they're
In there, and then some, some gloves, I didn't have a hat, but I don't know where it went,
So I guess I'll have to find the little flea, a little neoprene hat.
And so anyways, yeah, I think I can get enough clothing in here to keep me warm.
And that's my gear, that's how I go, it's all about the power bars, because you can get
Power bars for $0.80 each at Costco, so $0.80 for 10 to 40 calories, that's not bad.
Um, here, let me show you how I live really quick, I might do a specific video about it.
But this is, when I'm working on my computer, I just sit on this, and I can rest my hands
Up on this, and then just pedal, pedal, pedal away, and I can record all of it, and it's
Not too loud.
Um, so, um, that's that, peace out.

Here's my shorts, I bought them specifically, uh, lately, with the bibs, so I don't, so they

Because they have the, um, they have, like, the little bike shorts in here, you know, that go down

Running on your crotch, and I've got those for blisters for my feet, and I even remember to get
